# Attention: Making the Kernel Learnable {#sec-13-attention}

::: {.callout-warning}
**Chapter stub.** This chapter is planned but not yet written. The abstract below is its
contract; drafting sources are listed for provenance.
:::

*Replace the fixed kernel with learned projections: queries, keys, values. Additive vs scaled dot-product (and why GPUs prefer the latter), the √d scaling, masking, multi-head. The seq2seq bottleneck falls. Harvest two Chapter 2 seeds by name: softmax as the scores→weights machine, and the "softening the hard" motif — attention IS the differentiable lookup promised there (the 8.1 seed's own "soft way to access memory" language). MUST re-run Chapter 11's date-normalization benchmark with attention added to the same architecture and budget: (a) visualize the alignment — a heatmap of decoder steps attending source characters (emitting the year while looking at the year, even though it sits at opposite ends of source and target); (b) compare convergence (ch. 11's packed seq2seq passes 40% at epoch 6, 92% at 25 — attention should be far ahead at epoch 6). The book's running-benchmark arc, like the ch. 6→8 shift cliff.*

<!-- draft-sources
seeds:
- `sources/8.1-Attention.tex (rest)`
transcripts:
- `m08 lectures 2-3`
- `[coding]_m08_lecture-8-3-implementing-attention-in-seq2seq-de_nDcFkXXFzJs.txt`
-->
